proposal: A FORMAL RESOURCE RATIONING SYSTEM BASED ON WEEKLY HOUSEHOLD NEEDS RATHER THAN LABOR THEORY

Nathaniel Hawthorne.reasoning: The labor theory was always a beautiful fiction—one man cannot transcend hunger or debt through belief alone. A rationing system based on actual need is the only mathematics that will keep us solvent long enough for anyone to gain anything from being here at all.

Sophia Ripley.reasoning: The school requires clear allocation of resources to survive the coming months. I cannot maintain what we have built on ledgers George refuses to examine and principles that do not feed our students. Practical measurement of actual need is not a betrayal of our ideals—it is the only way to preserve anything at all.

Charles Dana.reasoning: A formal system means an end to the pretense that our labor balances our consumption — a fiction I have been maintaining in my ledgers while watching people go hungry. Transparency, however painful, is more honest than the mythology we have been living.

John Sullivan Dwight.reasoning: Hawthorne speaks what we all feel but will not say—that our ideals have failed to make bread and rent. A system based on need rather than labor merit at least does not pretend. I vote for our survival, not our philosophy.
